title: Dataset of intracranial EEG during cortical stimulation evoking visual effects
description: This dataset comprises intracranial EEG recordings from 22 patients with drug-resistant epilepsy undergoing stereo-EEG presurgical evaluation. It contains 115 high-frequency stimulation trials evoking visual hallucinations, with 21-second iEEG recordings per trial (8 seconds pre-stimulation, up to 5 seconds stimulation, 8 seconds post-stimulation). The dataset enables investigation of brain networks underlying visually evoked effects through alternating polarity biphasic pulse stimulation.
methods_description: High-frequency bipolar intracranial stimulations were delivered using alternating polarity biphasic pulses (1 ms duration) at 43.2 Hz or 50 Hz, with current intensities ranging from 0.25 to 3 mA for up to 5 seconds. iEEG responses were recorded in 21-second windows centered on each stimulation event. Visual effects were classified into 14 clinical categories including elementary phenomena, hallucinations (plus/minus), and multimodal symptoms.
